# `lang.erlang.runtime.otp` — Erlang/OTP behaviours

Auto-generated. Back to [summary](../summary.md).

- **Language:** [erlang](../by-language/erlang.md)
- **Category:** [language](../by-category/language.md)
- **Capability cells:** 1

## Capabilities

| Capability | Status | Verified at | Issue | Cites | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------------|-------|-------|-------|
| Core extraction | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-24` | 4929 | `internal/extractors/erlang/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/erlang/extractor_test.go`<br>`internal/extractors/erlang/otp_deepen.go`<br>`internal/types/kinds.go` | #4903: OTP behaviour detection. -behaviour(gen_server|gen_statem|gen_event|gen_fsm|supervisor|application). (both British -behaviour and American -behavior spellings; behaviourRE) is detected and stamps the module entity — Subtype refined to gen_server_module / supervisor_module / application_module (or otp_module for multi-behaviour modules; otpModuleSubtype), Properties["otp_behaviour"]=comma-list, Tags ["otp","otp:<behaviour>"]. The behaviour's canonical callbacks (handle_call/handle_cast/handle_info/init/terminate/code_change for gen_server; init for supervisor; start/stop for application; etc.; otpCallbacks table) are re-typed Subtype="otp_callback" with Properties["otp_callback_of"]. #4929 deepens this into two semantic layers, closing the previously-partial gap. (1) SUPERVISION-TREE CHILD_SPEC EDGES: when a module is a supervisor (behaviourSet["supervisor"]) its init/1 body is parsed for the child spec list and a SUPERVISES edge (new RelationshipKindSupervises in internal/types/kinds.go, registered in AllRelationshipKinds and proven by the producer-kinds guardrail in internal/types) is emitted from the supervisor module entity to each child module; parseChildSpecs handles BOTH spec shapes after stripCommentsAndStrings scrubbing: the modern map form (childSpecMapStartRE for the start-MFA module, childSpecMapIDRE for the id; map literals isolated by mapSegments so each start is paired with its own id) and the legacy tuple form {Id, {M, F, A}, ...} (childSpecTupleRE); children de-duplicated by module; edge Properties: child_id, provenance="otp_child_spec". (2) PER-MESSAGE-TAG DISPATCH: for the request-dispatching callbacks (handle_call/handle_cast/handle_info/handle_event/handle_sync_event; isDispatchCallback) the message TAG of each clause is recovered (first pattern element of the first arg; a {get, Key} tuple yields 'get', a bare atom 'flush' yields 'flush') from per-clause argument heads (funcInfo.argHeads; clauseMatch.args is funcHeadRE group 2) via firstArgTag + splitTopLevelFirst (nesting-aware top-level comma split over (){}[]) + isAtom; variable/wildcard catch-all clauses (_Request,_Msg,_) carry no concrete tag and are skipped; tags emitted in clause order, de-duplicated, onto the callback as Properties["otp_dispatch_tags"]=comma-list and Tags ["otp_msg:TAG", ...]. Proven by TestErlangExtractor_OTPBehaviour, TestErlangExtractor_OTPCallbacks, TestErlangExtractor_SupervisorBehaviour, TestErlangExtractor_SupervisionTreeEdges (cache_server + logger_srv maps + db_pool tuple), TestErlangExtractor_MessageTagDispatch (handle_call yields 'get'; handle_cast yields 'put,delete,flush'), TestErlangExtractor_GenServerRecall. #5363 (epic #5360) — OTP/BEAM DEEPENING (internal/extractors/erlang/otp_deepen.go). (A) CLIENT MESSAGE EDGES: gen_server/gen_statem/gen_event client send primitives (gen_server:call/cast, gen_statem:call/cast, gen_event:notify/call, gen_fsm:[sync_]send_event; otpSendRE+otpSendKind) are recovered per clause body and ENRICH the matching CALLS edge with otp_msg_kind (call|cast|notify), otp_msg_server (1st arg, ?SERVER/?MODULE-expanded), otp_msg_tag (2nd-arg message tag: {get,_}->get, bare flush->flush; otpSendServer/otpSendTag) and provenance=otp_client_send (enrichOTPMessageEdges); the function entity gets Tags otp_send:<kind> and otp_msg_out:<tag>. This is the CLIENT side of the gen_server protocol, pairing with the existing server-side otp_dispatch_tags. Proven by TestErlangExtractor_ClientMessageEdges (get->call/get, put->cast/put, flush->cast/flush on cache_server). (B) SUPERVISION RESTART STRATEGY: a supervisor init/1 SupFlags is parsed in BOTH forms — modern map #{strategy => one_for_one, intensity => 5, period => 10} (supStrategyMapRE/supIntensityMapRE/supPeriodMapRE) and legacy tuple {{one_for_one,5,10}, [Children]} (supStrategyTupleRE) — and stamped on the supervisor module entity as Properties otp_sup_strategy/otp_sup_intensity/otp_sup_period + Tag otp_sup_strategy:<strategy> (recoverSupFlags/stampSupFlags), so the supervision tree carries its restart semantics (one_for_one|one_for_all|rest_for_one|simple_one_for_one) alongside the SUPERVISES child topology. Proven by TestErlangExtractor_SupervisorRestartStrategy (map form one_for_one/5/10) and TestErlangExtractor_SupervisorLegacyStrategy (tuple form one_for_all/3/60). Mnesia/ETS/DETS table modelling lands as lang.erlang.core db_effect (same PR). |
